notes.Crown of Madness: Unreliable (the tooltip lies — it just applies the chaotic 'madness' AI and marks the target hostile to its allies), but when you isolate line-of-sight you can turn a boss's adds against it or make AOE enemies blow each other up.
notes.Detect Thoughts: Mediocre in dialogue, but it's a free (ritual) all-day concentration spell — the best way to hold a concentration for Strange Conduit / to fuel the Concentrated Blast illithid power without giving up a real spell.
notes.Enhance Ability: Advantage on a chosen ability's skill checks — mostly redundant with Friends/Gloves of Thieving, but invaluable (and reassuring to have prepared) for the high-value Act 3 checks that are otherwise hard to boost.
notes.Lesser Restoration: Removes paralysis/poison/blind/disease — a panic button against Hold Person (action-neutral, since the paralysed ally wasn't acting anyway) and it clears some all-day debuffs; you'll rarely cast it, but it's clutch when you do.
notes.Mirror Image: Pre-castable +9 AC (shedding 3 per enemy miss) — strong on its own, but doubly good because the AI won't target high-AC characters, so it protects a concentration-holder or makes an already-armoured character untouchable.
notes.Warding Bond: Splits an ally's damage with the caster (+1 AC/saves) and stacks with damage-reduction/resistance to actually lower total damage taken — strong on an Oath of the Crown paladin or a tanky front-liner (and broken via camp-casting).
notes.Web: Entangle for sorcerers/wizards — a 10-turn AOE root with a bigger radius and, helpfully, a Dexterity save (better vs the strength-based melee you most want to stop); same flammable downside.
notes.Arcane Lock: Permanently locks a door — niche, but luring half an encounter through a doorway and locking it buys turns (enemies now break it down rather than bugging out), and it cheeses a few story events.
notes.Enlarge/Reduce: A medium damage buff (Enlarge) or shrink enemies to throw them off cliffs / lower their strength saves, plus weight tricks for jump-down owlbear builds — a creative, situational toolbox spell.
notes.Knock: Opens any lock, but almost every party already has a Sleight of Hand character and the game is winnable without opening anything — a costly backup for lockless parties.
notes.Pass Without Trace: +10 stealth to the whole party — useless for most parties (you rarely roll stealth), but mandatory for the game-breaking greater-invisibility / stealth-archer strategy.
notes.Protection from Poison: All-day, no-concentration poison resistance + advantage vs the poisoned condition — only matters in the handful of poison-heavy fights (some Act 2 encounters), but very relevant there.
notes.Ray of Enfeeblement: Halves a strength-based attacker's weapon damage (con save to end), but it's a concentration debuff you'd rather spend on Hold Person and it whiffs on archers/finesse enemies — niche use is on debuff-immune bosses.
notes.Shatter: Low, con-save AOE thunder (no better than Chromatic Orb's thunder) — mainly useful to one-shot Scrying Eyes (they have disadvantage and thunder vulnerability) and for Tempest clerics to maximise.
notes.Barkskin: Sets AC to 16 (does nothing if you're already there) at the cost of concentration AND a slot — too weak and too expensive; just don't.
notes.Branding Smite: Half the damage of a same-slot Divine Smite, plus wasted concentration/bonus action for a useless anti-invisibility rider — the worst level-2 smite.
notes.Darkvision: See in (non-magical) dark out to 40ft — but most arenas are lit, many races have it innately, and you can just use a light source or a scroll. Don't spend a slot.
notes.Enthrall: Forces a creature to stare at you with essentially no mechanical effect — Fog Cloud and Minor Illusion do the steal-distraction job far better.
notes.Flame Blade: A 3d6 fire sword for a slot that doesn't add your modifier and can't be buffed — usually less than just swinging a real weapon, and druids only get one attack.
notes.Gust of Wind: A ranged line-push (and cloud-dispel) that's outclassed by free bonus-action shoves and is too short (40ft) to knock archers off high ground — cool, rarely useful.
notes.Melf's Acid Arrow: ~15 single-target acid damage — pathetic for a level-2 slot (less than Cloud of Daggers, with an attack roll); a damage spell that does no damage.
notes.Phantasmal Force: Butchered in translation — 1d6/turn (less than a cantrip) on an Intelligence save with repeated saves to escape, after losing its real tabletop effect entirely. Skip.
notes.Prayer of Healing: Efficient OUT-of-combat group healing — which BG3 never needs, since you can rest or chug potions freely. No use case outside a no-rest challenge.
notes.See Invisibility: Reveals invisible enemies on a dex save, but splashing water reveals them with no save, the game gives free See Invisibility and a pile of scrolls — don't spend a slot.
